target.title: Doctor: expose state integrity findings
target.body:
## Summary

- add structured `core/doctor/state-integrity` findings for state directory, config permission, storage-class, and runtime state directory issues
- preserve config-file permission findings even when the state directory itself is missing
- add dry-run repair effects for the same issue set while keeping real mutations owned by the existing legacy `noteStateIntegrity` path
- keep the Doctor contribution order intact and register the new structured check under `doctor:state-integrity`

## Validation

Current head `50feea3a2e`:

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts --reporter=dot` - 32 passed, 1 skipped on Windows
- Windows source CLI proof with temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`:
  - `corepack pnpm openclaw doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/state-integrity` - exit 1, emitted the structured `core/doctor/state-integrity` missing-state-dir finding
- Added regression: missing state dir plus permissive config file still maps both the `missing-state-dir` error and `config-file-too-open` warning on POSIX, while child runtime-dir checks remain gated on the state dir existing
- `node_modules\.bin\oxlint.CMD src\commands\doctor-state-integrity.ts src\commands\doctor-state-integrity.test.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.CMD --check src\commands\doctor-state-integrity.ts src\commands\doctor-state-integrity.test.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `node node_modules\@typescript\native-preview\bin\tsgo.js -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts\tsgo-cache\core.tsbuildinfo --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `node node_modules\@typescript\native-preview\bin\tsgo.js -p test\tsconfig\tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts\tsgo-cache\test-src.tsbuildinfo --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`

Previous head `c39ead5c60` also passed the broader focused shard before the review fix: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=dot`.

## Notes

- No public SDK/plugin/config contract changes; the package surface guard reports `package-exported forbidden subpaths: 0`.
- POSIX permission-mode behavior is covered by the new regression. A local Linux source CLI proof was not run from this Windows host because WSL has no Linux Node install and the local Crabbox wrapper failed its binary sanity check; the PR remains draft pending reviewer/CI confirmation.
- The Windows source CLI proof cannot show `config-file-too-open` because Windows intentionally skips POSIX mode diagnostics.
target.author: giodl73-repo
